real_questions: Did appetites change during particular periods of market volatility?
Do you anticipate appetites to change when rates come down or go back up?
How do you manage risks of growing fast?
What happens when growth slows and you start to reach for growth?
On enterprise risk management, how has the risk management infrastructure or headcount grown relative to overall growth?
Are there any efficiencies from AI?
What is the starting point for your view on credit spreads?
agency_intel: Moody's watches whether risk infrastructure / headcount has kept up with the firm's growth rate. AI efficiencies are a current topic — expect probing on what's being deployed and where the discipline trade-offs sit.

Moody's applies the heaviest qualitative overlay of the three agencies. Analysts have real discretion to adjust a historical ratio's implied sub-score based on forward projections and peer positioning. The factors they lean on: concentration risk above all (they can double-hit an issuer over concentration), plus complexity and diversification.

Moody's penalizes complexity and opacity — if there are parts of the business they don't understand, that counts against you. More than any other agency, Moody's wants to see the business simplified. Come prepared to explain concentration and forward concentration expectations clearly.
